stories.title: Repo Skeleton: README.md, AGENTS.md stub, and top-level structure
stories.description: Create the top-level repo layout: README.md (human-oriented overview, quick start, links), CHANGELOG.md (v1.0.0 [WIRE] entry), VERSIONING.md (semver policy), and AGENTS.md stub with all 5 required section headings. The repo already has LICENSE.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md exists and contains: repo purpose, 3-step quick start, links to SPEC.md, AGENTS.md, and conformance/
CHANGELOG.md exists with a v1.0.0 [WIRE] entry
VERSIONING.md exists with MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH definitions and a note that downstream SDKs MUST declare the spec version they implement
AGENTS.md exists with all 5 required section headings (stub content acceptable at this stage)
All Markdown files are valid (no broken link anchors, no unclosed fences)

qualityChecks.command: npx markdownlint-cli2 README.md CHANGELOG.md VERSIONING.md AGENTS.md
qualityChecks.description: Validate Markdown syntax for all top-level docs

stories.title: SPEC.md: Full Normative Prose Specification
stories.description: Write the full normative SPEC.md covering all 15 required sections: problem statement, source-of-truth strategy, server-side requirements, public API surface (all 7 methods), constructor options table with exact error strings, env enum with wire values and behavioral table, HTTP wire protocol (endpoint, headers, request body, response, timeout, error taxonomy with 4 categories, sampling), ID generation, schema extraction algorithm (pseudocode + key invariants), 13 golden fixtures inline, deduplication (OPTIONAL, 500ms window, key formula, manual cross-bucket note), encryption (ECIES P-256, byte layout, KDF), keepalive/flush, open questions, out of scope.
acceptanceCriteria: SPEC.md exists and contains all 15 required sections
All normative requirements use RFC 2119 MUST/SHOULD/MAY language
Constructor validation table is present with exact error message strings for apiKey and version
Error taxonomy table has all 4 categories with correct promise outcomes: network timeout/error resolve with eventSchema, SDK internal error rejects with fixed string, non-200 resolves with []
Schema extraction algorithm pseudocode matches the 13 golden fixtures
libVersion wire format is specified as plain SemVer string (no +spec suffix)
Dedup section notes that cross-bucket multi-step conformance requires manual testing
flush() section is prefixed with a note that it is a new requirement not present in the node reference SDK
0.0 classification is documented as float in statically-typed languages with JS-specific Number.isInteger guidance

qualityChecks.command: npx markdownlint-cli2 SPEC.md
qualityChecks.description: Validate Markdown syntax

qualityChecks.command: wc -w SPEC.md | awk '{if($1 < 3000) {print "FAIL: SPEC.md too short (" $1 " words)"; exit 1} else print "OK: " $1 " words"}'
qualityChecks.description: Ensure SPEC.md is comprehensive (minimum 3000 words)

qualityChecks.command: grep -c "^##" SPEC.md | awk '{if($1 < 10) {print "FAIL: too few sections in SPEC.md (found " $1 ")"; exit 1} else print "OK: " $1 " sections"}'
qualityChecks.description: Verify at least 10 top-level sections exist — a thin SPEC.md with too few sections fails all downstream stories

qualityChecks.command: grep -q "MUST\|SHOULD\|MAY" SPEC.md || (echo "FAIL: No RFC 2119 language found" && exit 1) && grep -q "extractSchema\|trackSchemaFromEvent" SPEC.md || (echo "FAIL: API methods not found" && exit 1) && grep -q "ECIES\|encryption" SPEC.md || (echo "FAIL: Encryption section missing" && exit 1) && grep -q "500.*ms\|dedup" SPEC.md || (echo "FAIL: Dedup section missing" && exit 1) && echo "OK: all critical sections present"
qualityChecks.description: Verify critical section content (RFC 2119 language, API methods, encryption, dedup) — catches a structurally valid but substantively incomplete SPEC.md

stories.title: openapi.yaml and schemas/ Directory
stories.description: Write the OpenAPI 3.1 document for POST /inspector/v1/track and the 7 JSON Schema files under schemas/: event-batch.json, event-body.json, event-body-encrypted.json, base-body.json, event-property-plain.json, event-property-encrypted.json, schema-entry.json. These are the machine-readable contracts AI agents use for wire-compatible serialization code.
acceptanceCriteria: openapi.yaml is valid OpenAPI 3.1
All 7 schema files exist under schemas/
env enum in event-body.json contains exactly ["dev", "staging", "prod"]
libVersion pattern in event-body.json matches plain SemVer (no +spec suffix) — regex: ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$
messageId pattern in event-body.json matches UUID v4 format
eventProperties in event-body.json references event-property-plain.json
eventProperties in event-body-encrypted.json references event-property-encrypted.json
All JSON Schema files are valid JSON Schema draft 2020-12 (not just valid JSON)

qualityChecks.command: npx @redocly/cli lint openapi.yaml
qualityChecks.description: Validate OpenAPI 3.1 spec

qualityChecks.command: for f in schemas/*.json; do python3 -m json.tool $f > /dev/null && echo "valid: $f" || echo "INVALID: $f"; done
qualityChecks.description: Validate all JSON Schema files are valid JSON (syntax check)

qualityChecks.command: npx ajv-cli compile -s schemas/event-body.json schemas/event-batch.json schemas/event-body-encrypted.json schemas/base-body.json schemas/event-property-plain.json schemas/event-property-encrypted.json schemas/schema-entry.json
qualityChecks.description: Validate JSON Schema draft 2020-12 compliance — python3 -m json.tool only checks JSON syntax, not schema validity; invalid $ref paths or keyword usage would pass syntax check but fail here

qualityChecks.command: python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('schemas/event-body.json')); pat=s.get('properties',{}).get('libVersion',{}).get('pattern',''); assert pat==r'^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$', f'FAIL: libVersion pattern is {pat}'; print('OK: libVersion pattern matches plain SemVer')"
qualityChecks.description: Assert libVersion pattern in event-body.json is plain SemVer (no +spec suffix)

stories.title: conformance/schema-extraction/fixtures.json (Fixtures 1–13)
stories.description: Write the 13 golden schema extraction conformance fixtures as machine-readable JSON. Each fixture has fixture_id, description, input, and expected fields. Includes conformance/README.md and conformance/schema-extraction/README.md. NOTE: conformance/batching/ is explicitly OUT OF V1 SCOPE — do NOT create it.
acceptanceCriteria: conformance/schema-extraction/fixtures.json contains exactly 13 fixtures with IDs fixture-1 through fixture-13
Each fixture has fixture_id, description, input, and expected fields
fixture-3: 0.0 expected type is "float", {} expected has propertyType object with children [], [] expected has propertyType list(string) with children []
fixture-7: children array is ["float", "string", [{"propertyName": "three", "propertyType": "int"}]]
fixture-8: input is null, expected is []
fixture-13: input is {"a": {"b": {"c": 42}}} with 3-level nested children
All fixture JSON is valid (parseable)
conformance/README.md and conformance/schema-extraction/README.md exist
conformance/batching/ directory MUST NOT be created (batching is out of v1 scope)

qualityChecks.command: python3 -m json.tool conformance/schema-extraction/fixtures.json > /dev/null && echo valid
qualityChecks.description: Validate fixtures JSON is parseable

qualityChecks.command: python3 -c "import json; f=json.load(open('conformance/schema-extraction/fixtures.json')); assert len(f)==13, f'Expected 13 fixtures, got {len(f)}'; print('OK: 13 fixtures found')"
qualityChecks.description: Assert exactly 13 fixtures exist

qualityChecks.command: python3 -c "import json; f=json.load(open('conformance/schema-extraction/fixtures.json')); f3=next(x for x in f if x['fixture_id']=='fixture-3'); d_prop=next(x for x in f3['expected'] if x['propertyName']=='d'); assert d_prop['propertyType']=='float', f'FAIL: 0.0 classified as {d_prop[\"propertyType\"]} not float'; print('OK: 0.0 correctly classified as float')"
qualityChecks.description: Assert fixture-3 classifies 0.0 as float not int — highest-likelihood content deviation from node SDK reference implementation

stories.title: conformance/wire-protocol/fixtures.json (Fixtures wire-1 through wire-5)
stories.description: Write the 5 wire-protocol conformance fixtures: wire-1 (basic event send), wire-2 (sampling drop with no HTTP call), wire-3 (non-200 response resolves), wire-4 (streamId with colon — spec Edge Case 9), wire-5 (empty streamId treated as no stream ID — spec Edge Case 10). Includes conformance/wire-protocol/README.md documenting AVO_INSPECTOR_MOCK_ENDPOINT and format validation patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: conformance/wire-protocol/fixtures.json contains fixtures wire-1, wire-2, wire-3, wire-4, wire-5
wire-1 expected_request_body contains "<uuid-v4>", "<iso8601>", "<semver>", "<sdk-platform>" placeholders
wire-1 expected_request_body includes avoFunction: false, eventId: null, eventHash: null
wire-2 has expected_request_count: 0 and mock_response: null
wire-3 notes field does NOT contain the ambiguous "or the extracted schema" clause
wire-4 expected_request_body includes anonymousId: "stream:with:colons" (verbatim colon passthrough, spec Edge Case 9)
wire-5 expected_request_body includes anonymousId: "" (empty string passthrough, spec Edge Case 10)
conformance/wire-protocol/README.md documents AVO_INSPECTOR_MOCK_ENDPOINT and format validation regex patterns
All JSON is valid

qualityChecks.command: python3 -m json.tool conformance/wire-protocol/fixtures.json > /dev/null && echo valid
qualityChecks.description: Validate wire-protocol fixtures JSON is parseable

stories.title: conformance/deduplication and conformance/error-handling Fixtures
stories.description: Write deduplication fixtures (marked OPTIONAL) and error-handling fixtures. Dedup README must note single-invocation limitation for cross-bucket scenarios. Error-handling fixtures cover sampling boundary, non-200 response, and empty eventProperties.
acceptanceCriteria: conformance/deduplication/fixtures.json exists with at least 2 fixtures
conformance/deduplication/README.md explicitly states dedup conformance is OPTIONAL and notes single-invocation limitation for cross-bucket scenarios
conformance/error-handling/fixtures.json exists with at least 3 fixtures
error-2 fixture has expected_promise_outcome: "resolve" (non-200 must not reject)
All JSON files are valid

qualityChecks.command: python3 -m json.tool conformance/deduplication/fixtures.json > /dev/null && echo valid
qualityChecks.description: Validate dedup fixtures JSON

qualityChecks.command: python3 -m json.tool conformance/error-handling/fixtures.json > /dev/null && echo valid
qualityChecks.description: Validate error-handling fixtures JSON

stories.title: conformance/runner-contract.md (Normative Harness Protocol)
stories.description: Write conformance/runner-contract.md — the complete normative harness protocol. No TBD sections allowed. Covers: entry point, invocation protocol, input/output envelope schemas, exit codes, AVO_INSPECTOR_MOCK_ENDPOINT env var, format validation patterns with regex, mock server protocol, and implementation checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: conformance/runner-contract.md exists
All 10 required sections are present with no TBD placeholders
Input envelope schema documents all fields including precondition
Format validation patterns table includes all 4 rows with regex strings
AVO_INSPECTOR_MOCK_ENDPOINT is documented
Exit codes 0/1/2 are defined
No TBD sections anywhere in the file

qualityChecks.command: npx markdownlint-cli2 conformance/runner-contract.md
qualityChecks.description: Validate Markdown syntax

qualityChecks.command: grep -i 'tbd\|to be defined\|todo' conformance/runner-contract.md && echo 'FAIL: TBD sections found' && exit 1 || echo 'OK: no TBD sections'
qualityChecks.description: Ensure no TBD or TODO sections remain

qualityChecks.command: for section in "Entry point" "Invocation protocol" "Input envelope" "Output envelope" "Exit codes" "AVO_INSPECTOR_MOCK_ENDPOINT" "Format validation" "Mock server" "Implementation checklist"; do grep -q "$section" conformance/runner-contract.md || (echo "FAIL: Missing section: $section" && exit 1); done && echo "OK: all required sections present"
qualityChecks.description: Verify all 9 critical section keywords are present — markdownlint only validates syntax, not content completeness; a missing section breaks harness implementors

stories.title: AGENTS.md: Complete AI Agent SDK Generation Guide
stories.description: Replace the AGENTS.md stub with the complete version. Must contain all 5 required sections: (1) What to build, (2) Files to read in order, (3) SDK generation checklist (minimum 12 binary pass/fail items including flush for non-Node, enableLogging process-wide, exact wire body fields), (4) How to run conformance with exact command, (5) Definition of done referencing all 19 acceptance criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: AGENTS.md contains all 5 required sections

Section 2 lists files in the correct reading order: AGENTS.md → SPEC.md → openapi.yaml → schemas/ → conformance/runner-contract.md → conformance/schema-extraction/fixtures.json → conformance/wire-protocol/fixtures.json
Section 3 contains at least 12 binary pass/fail checklist items
Section 3 includes the flush() requirement for non-Node SDKs
Section 3 includes the enableLogging process-wide requirement
Section 4 contains the exact string: echo '<fixture-json>' | avo-inspector-conformance
Section 5 references all 19 acceptance criteria in SPEC.md
No ambiguous language ("should", "might", "probably") — only RFC 2119 MUST/SHOULD/MAY or concrete imperatives

qualityChecks.command: npx markdownlint-cli2 AGENTS.md
qualityChecks.description: Validate Markdown syntax

qualityChecks.command: grep -c '\[ \]' AGENTS.md | awk '{if($1 < 12) {print "FAIL: too few checklist items in AGENTS.md (found " $1 ")"; exit 1} else print "OK: " $1 " checklist items"}'
qualityChecks.description: Verify Section 3 SDK generation checklist has at least 12 binary pass/fail items

stories.title: CHANGELOG.md and VERSIONING.md Final Pass
stories.description: Rewrite CHANGELOG.md and VERSIONING.md with complete content. CHANGELOG.md gets a header explaining [WIRE]/[SPEC] tags and a complete v1.0.0 [WIRE] entry listing all normative wire-protocol sections. VERSIONING.md gets the full semver policy, regeneration rules, and spec version declaration guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANGELOG.md has a complete v1.0.0 entry tagged [WIRE]
CHANGELOG.md header explains the [WIRE]/[SPEC] distinction
VERSIONING.md defines MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH with downstream SDK regeneration requirements
VERSIONING.md explains how generated SDKs should declare the spec version
Both files are valid Markdown

qualityChecks.command: npx markdownlint-cli2 CHANGELOG.md VERSIONING.md
qualityChecks.description: Validate Markdown syntax for both files
